items.title: Stock Radar: City Union Bank stock hits fresh record high in February; time to buy or book profits?
items.description: City Union Bank stock is showing strong upward momentum. Experts predict the rally will continue, with a potential target of Rs 321. The stock has broken out of a long-term bullish pattern and is trading above key moving averages. This indicates a positive outlook for the banking stock in the coming weeks. Traders can consider buying on dips.

items.title: HAL shares fall 12% in 2 days, PSU clarifies on Tejas fighter jets and guidance
items.description: HAL shares fell sharply, extending a sharp two-day decline, after concerns over Tejas Mk1A delivery delays and rising private-sector competition triggered investor anxiety. The company issued a clarification, confirming five jets are ready while nine await GE engines, and reaffirmed its full-year guidance despite losing ground in India’s AMCA program.

items.title: Devyani International shares soar 8% on Jefferies buy upgrade, Rs 145 target unchanged
items.description: Devyani International shares surged after Jefferies upgraded its rating to 'Buy', citing a 17% correction and a target price implying 25% upside. The company announced Manish Dawar's promotion to CEO, ensuring leadership continuity. Despite mixed Q3 results, with KFC India improving sequentially and Pizza Hut India declining, the company added 95 net new stores.

items.title: ​HDFC Bank among 6 largecap stocks with upside potential of up to 33%
items.description: After a corrective phase lasting over a year, experts say largecaps remain the preferred choice for long-term portfolios. Axis Direct believes select opportunities are now emerging across the space. In a recent report, the brokerage has identified six stocks across multiple sectors that offer attractive value and could potentially deliver returns of up to a staggering 33% over the next year. Here’s the full list.

items.title: Hindustan Copper shares fall over 5%. What’s behind the sharp selloff?
items.description: Hindustan Copper shares tumbled 5.5% as global copper prices reversed sharply, driven by renewed concerns over Chinese demand. Investors are unwinding bullish bets amid signs of weakening spot demand in China and rising inventories, despite copper's year-to-date gains.

items.title: Hindustan Zinc shares tank 7%, snap 3-day streak as silver prices plummet 9%. Should you buy this dip?
items.description: Hindustan Zinc shares dropped significantly due to a sharp decline in silver prices and increased margins on MCX futures. Despite short-term volatility, analysts like HSBC remain optimistic, upgrading the stock with a higher target price, citing the company's strong fundamentals and positive outlook for zinc and silver.

items.title: F&O Radar: Bull Call spread strategy for Titan shares this week
items.description: Titan maintains a strong bullish structure, rebounding from its 20-week EMA and breaking above Rs 4,000. A close above Rs 4,200 may ignite the next uptrend toward Rs 4,350–4,400. Technical indicators remain positive, while a bull call spread strategy suggests favourable risk-reward for positional traders.

items.title: Qualcomm shares slide 9% as memory chip shortage hits smartphone market
items.description: Chip maker Qualcomm anticipates lower revenue and profit. A global memory chip shortage is affecting smartphone sales. This shortage is expected to continue for several quarters. Qualcomm's smartphone chip sales are projected to decrease. However, its data center business remains unaffected. The company is expanding into new areas to diversify.

items.title: India-US Trade Pact 2026: What It Means for Indian Stocks and Your Portfolio
items.description: India’s stock market staged a blockbuster rally after the landmark India-US trade deal, with the Sensex surging over 4,000 points and the Nifty jumping nearly 5%. The pact is being seen as a game-changer that could reverse massive FII outflows, boost investor sentiment, and trigger a re-rating across export-driven sectors. In this video, we break down what the deal means for Indian equities, which sectors stand to benefit the most, and why experts believe foreign money could soon return to Dalal Street.

items.title: Sensex falls over 400 pts, Nifty below 25,650; metal, pharma stocks bleed
items.description: Benchmark indices Nifty and Sensex opened lower on Thursday, ending a three-day rally as investors booked profits at higher levels. Metal and pharma stocks were the biggest decliners, with the broader market also experiencing a downturn. The BSE Sensex dropped over 200 points, while the Nifty 50 fell below 25,700.

items.title: Tata Power shares fall 2% after Q3 revenue declines 4% YoY to Rs 14,485 crore
items.description: Tata Power shares experienced a dip on Thursday after mixed Q3 FY26 results. Revenue fell 4% YoY to Rs 14,485 crore, while PAT rose 1% to Rs 1,194 crore. Renewables drove strong gains, with segment PAT up 156%. The company executed 1.3 GW of green projects, pushing cumulative renewable capacity past 10 GW.

items.title: ​Stocks to buy in 2026 for long term: Adani Ports, Ventive Hospitality among 5 stocks that could give 10-40% return
items.description: Brokerages are turning selective yet optimistic on a mix of infrastructure, consumer, industrial and hospitality stocks, pointing to meaningful upside from current levels.We have collated a list of recommendations from top brokerage firms from ETNow and other sources:

items.title: Buy, Sell or Hold: Motilal Oswal maintains buy on Castrol India; Nuvama recommends buy on Devyani International
items.description: Brokerages maintain a constructive outlook on select consumer and QSR stocks, citing margin resilience and growth potential. Castrol India and Emami are favored for their operational strengths, while Devyani International shows long-term expansion promise despite near-term challenges. Analysts highlight earnings recovery and significant upside potential.

items.title: Force Motors shares rally 8% as Q3 net profit spikes 250% to Rs 406 crore
items.description: Force Motors shares surged over 8% following a significant 252% jump in Q3 net profit to Rs 406.1 crore. This rise was fueled by a 12.6% increase in revenue to Rs 2,128 crore and a substantial 61.4% growth in EBITDA, alongside a one-time gain of Rs 211 crore.

items.title: Marico shares in focus on acquiring wellness brand for Rs 375 crore. Here’s everything you need to know
items.description: FMCG major Marico is set to acquire a 60% stake in plant-based nutrition brand Cosmix Wellness for approximately Rs 375 crore. This strategic move strengthens Marico's digital-first portfolio and positions it in the rapidly growing wellness segment. The acquisition aims to accelerate Cosmix's growth and expand into adjacent wellness categories.

items.title: Anthropic AI plug-ins creating chaos in IT stocks. Too early for you to plug out?
items.description: Indian IT stocks are experiencing significant selling pressure following the launch of AI-powered workplace tools, leading to a market value evaporation of billions. While some analysts label this a "SaaSpocalypse," others argue the market is overreacting, with AI potentially driving transformation rather than outright decline for the sector.

items.title: Tax increase shakes India’s $36 billion arbitrage trade
items.description: India's arbitrage fund managers anticipate reduced returns following a surprise tax hike on equity derivatives. This move, aimed at curbing speculation, impacts arbitrage funds by increasing operational costs and potentially lowering investor yields. While the sector has seen significant growth, the tax change could affect strategies and profitability, though some experts believe the market's dynamic nature may offer some mitigation.

items.title: For moderate risk-takers with medium-term perspective: 6 large-caps from different sectors with an upside potential of up to 28%
items.description: Our base case is that underlying sentiment is now positive. Going forward, there is a probability that the market may see some serious comeback attempts by the bulls. Now, this may not happen soon. Why? Because, normally, liquidity tends to be tight in the months of February and March. But in a medium- to long-term perspective, the probability of the bulls returning has increased. There are, however, many changes happening in the underlying businesses in many sectors. So investors need to be selective – and also cautious.

items.title: Japan's Nikkei falls as tech shares drag; Astellas surges
items.description: Japan's Nikkei index saw red on Thursday as technology stocks faltered, raising flags over the hefty investments tied to Artificial Intelligence. The decline was accentuated by a steep fall in shares of SoftBank Group, following Arm Holdings' disappointing revenue report. In contrast, the pharmaceuticals sector maintained its resilience, spurred by an optimistic forecast from Astellas Pharma which uplifted its stock.

items.title: IOC Q3 results: Profit jumps over 4x YoY to Rs 12,126 crore; revenue rises 7%
items.description: Indian Oil Corporation reported a strong rebound in Q3FY26, with profit after tax surging over four-fold to Rs 12,126 crore, aided by higher refining margins and improved operations. Revenue rose 7% to Rs 2.31 lakh crore, driven by petroleum products, petrochemicals, and gas, highlighting a sharp turnaround from last year’s muted earnings.

items.title: Sensex tumbles over 500 pts, Nifty set to snap 3-day gaining streak. 5 triggers behind the decline
items.description: Benchmark indices Nifty and Sensex slipped sharply on Thursday, poised to end a three-day winning streak as metals, IT, and global tech sentiment weighed on markets. A broad selloff in metals, profit booking after recent gains, weak cues from US and Asian equities, and caution ahead of the RBI policy dragged sentiment lower.

items.title: Buy stocks or follow grandmother's wisdom of investing in gold? What BlackRock CEO said
items.description: BlackRock CEO Larry Fink said gold remains a reliable diversifier but argued that long-term wealth creation lies in Indian equities, which he believes could double or triple over the next two decades. While acknowledging “grandmother’s wisdom” behind gold buying, he noted that investing in equities supports India’s economic growth in a way gold cannot.

items.title: Retail investors bet big on these 11 small-caps, 5 turn multibaggers
items.description: While market headlines often focus on institutional moves, retail investors have been steadily making their presence felt in the small-cap space. An analysis of December-quarter shareholding data reveals that retail investors have increased their stakes in nearly 386 small-cap stocks for two consecutive quarters, signalling growing confidence in select smaller companies. (Retail investors include resident individuals holding nominal share capital of up to Rs 2 lakh.Of the stocks where retail ownership has risen, around 40 small-cap companies have delivered share price gains of over 20% in the past year, outperforming broader market indices. More strikingly, 11 stocks from this list have surged between 50% and 370% over the same period, rewarding patient retail investors. Among them, five stocks emerged as true multibaggers, more than doubling investor wealth within just a year. (Data Source: ACE Equity)

items.title: IndiGo shares trim most of early losses, end nearly 1% lower
items.description: Shares of InterGlobe Aviation ended nearly 1 per cent lower on Thursday after the Competition Commission ordered a detailed probe against IndiGo for unfair business practices.

items.title: Two Trades for Today: A private sector power major for a 7% gain, a mid-cap IT stock for a 5% rise
items.description: Our technical analysts identify select stocks that may gain momentum even in volatile markets. Here are the technical calls for today.

items.title: Gainers & Losers: Suzlon Energy, Nykaa among 6 stocks in focus on Thursday
Weekly Top Picks: These stocks scored 10 on 10 on Stock Reports Plus
items.description: Stock Reports Plus, powered by Refinitiv, undertakes detailed company analysis for 4,000+ listed stocks. In addition to detailed company analyses, the report also collates analysts’ forecasts and trend analysis for each component. An average score in Stock Reports Plus is calculated by undertaking quantitative analysis of five key investment tools – earnings, fundamentals, relative valuation, risk, and price momentum.

items.title: These large- and mid-cap stocks can give more than 25% return in 1 year, according to analysts
items.description: From October 2024 till as recently as February 1, 2026, we mostly heard two words on the stock market: Bearish and volatile. The last few days have been different. For a change, we are hearing things like “positive market breadth” and “gains”. As the bulls attempt another serious comeback, the market again faces a familiar question: Are valuations still too high? Or have they reached reasonable levels after more than a year of correction? The answer is in the grey area. It’s a mixed bag really – and may differ from sector to sector.

items.title: ECB keeps rates unchanged even as choppy markets threaten its 'good place'
items.description: The European Central Bank left interest rates unchanged as expected on Thursday and offered no clues about its ⁠next move, reinforcing market bets that policy will remain steady for some time as the bloc enjoys steady growth and near-target inflation.
